channel.title: NPR Topics: Culture
channel.description: Art and entertainment commentary plus interviews, book reviews, movie reviews, music reviews, comedy, and visual art. Subscribe to podcasts and follow trends in music, painting, art, architecture, photography, and design.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: 'Wait Wait' for April 18. 2026: With Not My Job guest Phil Pritchard
item.description: This week, Wait Wait is live in Chicago with host Peter Sagal, guest judge and scorekeeper Alzo Slade, special guest Phil Pritchard and panelists Alonzo Bodden, Adam Burke, and Dulcé Sloan
item.content:encoded:
This week, Wait Wait is live in Chicago with host Peter Sagal, guest judge and scorekeeper Alzo Slade, special guest Phil Pritchard and panelists Alonzo Bodden, Adam Burke, and Dulcé Sloan

(Image credit: Bruce Bennett)

item.title: Photos: How overfishing in Southeast Asia is an ecological and human crisis
item.description: A rare look at one of the world's most critical and understudied environmental crises. Southeast Asia produces more than half of the world's fish, yet its waters are among the most depleted and contested.
item.content:encoded:
A rare look at one of the world's most critical and understudied environmental crises. Southeast Asia produces more than half of the world's fish, yet its waters are among the most depleted and contested.

(Image credit: Nicole Tung)
item.dc:creator: Nicole Tung

item.title: Sitting in a jail cell, alone and hopeless, a man's life is suddenly changed
item.description: Jay found himself sitting in a jail cell, alone and hopeless, after attempting to break into a building on his college campus. A fellow inmate's unexpected words brought him comfort — and changed the course of his life.
item.content:encoded:
Jay found himself sitting in a jail cell, alone and hopeless, after attempting to break into a building on his college campus. A fellow inmate's unexpected words brought him comfort — and changed the course of his life.

(Image credit: Irkham Khalid)
item.dc:creator: Autumn Barnes

item.title: While in a detention cell, a young man heard a voice that allowed him to see hope
item.description: A man recalls the despair he felt when, as a college student, he tried to break into a building on campus and ended up in a detention cell. That's when he heard a voice that helped him find hope.
item.content:encoded: A man recalls the despair he felt when, as a college student, he tried to break into a building on campus and ended up in a detention cell. That's when he heard a voice that helped him find hope.

item.title: You can't fake this: 'The Christophers' is a witty film about forgery and friendship
item.description: In Steven Soderbergh's new dark comedy, Ian McKellen plays a famous painter, and Michaela Coel is an art restorer hired to infiltrate his home by his greedy grown-up children.
item.content:encoded:
In Steven Soderbergh's new dark comedy, Ian McKellen plays a famous painter, and Michaela Coel is an art restorer hired to infiltrate his home by his greedy grown-up children.

(Image credit: Claudette Barius)
item.dc:creator: Justin Chang

item.title: Filmmaker John Waters -- aka the 'Pope of Trash' -- turns 80
item.description: Once called the "King of Bad Taste," Waters is known for his off-beat cult films Pink Flamingos and Polyester, as well as the more mainstream Hairspray. Originally broadcast in 2014 and 2019.
item.content:encoded: Once called the "King of Bad Taste," Waters is known for his off-beat cult films Pink Flamingos and Polyester, as well as the more mainstream Hairspray. Originally broadcast in 2014 and 2019.
item.dc:creator: Terry Gross

item.title: A new documentary on Lorne Michaels reveals plenty — except the man himself
item.description: Morgan Neville's film is packed with access and celebrity voices, but avoids deeper questions about its subject.
item.content:encoded:
Lorne.'/>

Morgan Neville's film is packed with access and celebrity voices, but avoids deeper questions about its subject.
item.dc:creator: Eric Deggans

item.title: Singer D4vd is arrested months after a teen's remains were found in his car
item.description: The 21-year-old Houston-born singer, whose real name is David Burke, had been under a secret investigation by an LA County grand jury after a 14-year-old girl's decomposed body was found in his car.
item.content:encoded:
The 21-year-old Houston-born singer, whose real name is David Burke, had been under a secret investigation by an LA County grand jury after a 14-year-old girl's decomposed body was found in his car.

(Image credit: Jordan Strauss/Invision/AP)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: A jury declared Live Nation a monopoly. But ticket prices won't drop just yet
item.description: D.C. and 33 states now have to argue in favor of specific remedies and fines, which could be paused if Live Nation appeals. Experts say the long-term impact on ticket prices isn't clear either.
item.content:encoded:
D.C. and 33 states now have to argue in favor of specific remedies and fines, which could be paused if Live Nation appeals. Experts say the long-term impact on ticket prices isn't clear either.

(Image credit: Paul Sakuma)
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: Boiling milk and worrying about the Iran war: A New Year dawns in Sri Lanka
item.description: In Sri Lanka, Buddhists and Hindus marked their New Year on Tuesday while a war thousands of miles away is making itself felt.
item.content:encoded:
In Sri Lanka, Buddhists and Hindus marked their New Year on Tuesday while a war thousands of miles away is making itself felt.

(Image credit: Sanka Vidanagama)
item.dc:creator: Diaa Hadid
